content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.2 WARN — release_notes.txt explicitly calls the App Store build a "public beta" and says "still testing the new sync feature, so expect rough edges" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/release_notes.txt:1-2), which is beta/unfinished language on store-facing copy.
Pierre: Apple reviewers treat release notes as store-facing copy, and language like "public beta" or "expect rough edges" signals the build may not be a finished, App Store-ready product, which can trigger a 2.2 rejection even though this text isn't in the binary itself. The description.txt is clean, but the release notes contradict it. Fix by rewriting the release notes to describe the sync feature as a normal shipped feature (e.g. "This update adds task sync across devices") without beta/testing disclaimers, and use TestFlight for actual pre-release testing.
